sql_array: CREATE TABLE test_data (id INT PRIMARY KEY, angle DOUBLE, url_value STRING);
INSERT INTO test_data (id, angle, url_value) VALUES (1, 1.4, 'https://example.com'), (2, 0, 'invalid_url'), (3, 3.14159, 'https://test.com/'), (4, -1.5, 'https://monetdb.org');
SELECT id, sin(angle), url_value FROM test_data WHERE angle IS NOT NULL AND url_value IS NOT NULL;
SELECT id, sin(angle) AS sine_value, url_value FROM test_data WHERE sin(angle) > 0.5 AND url 'https://valid.url' IS NOT NULL;
SELECT id, GROUP_CONCAT(url_value) AS concatenated_urls FROM (SELECT id, url_value FROM test_data WHERE sin(angle) < 0) AS subquery GROUP BY id HAVING COUNT(*) > 0;
SELECT id, angle, sin(angle) * 100 AS sin_scaled, url 'https://dynamic.url/' || id FROM test_data WHERE angle BETWEEN -2 AND 2 ORDER BY sin_scaled;
WITH RECURSIVE url_cte AS (SELECT url_value FROM test_data WHERE id = 1 UNION ALL SELECT url_value FROM test_data WHERE angle > 0) SELECT * FROM url_cte;
SELECT id, url 'https://example.org/' || (SELECT sin(MAX(angle)) FROM test_data) AS url_combination FROM test_data WHERE id = 2;
ALTER TABLE test_data ADD CONSTRAINT chk_angle CHECK (angle BETWEEN -3.14 AND 3.14);
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_table AS SELECT id, url_value FROM test_data WHERE angle < 0.5;
SELECT id, sin(angle) FROM temp_table WHERE url_value IS NULL;
SELECT id, url 'https://localhost/' || CAST(NULL AS STRING) AS malformed_url FROM test_data;
SELECT id, sin(angle) FROM test_data WHERE angle IN (SELECT MAX(angle) FROM test_data WHERE url_value IS NOT NULL) ORDER BY sin(angle) DESC;
